name: node-util-deprecate
title: node-util-deprecate
description:
Node.js's `util.deprecate()` function with browser support
In Node.js, this module simply re-exports the util.deprecate() function.
.
In the web browser (i.e. via browserify), a browser-specific
implementation of the util.deprecate() function is used.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-util-deprecate because Node.js's `util.deprecate()` function with browser support.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-util-deprecate as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-util-deprecate with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-util-deprecate --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
